mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized real-economy slowdown that overwhelms monetary policy transmission, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined index of INDPRO and HOUST declines shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent SPX 60-day returns (p > 0.10), or forward returns remain positive in > 55% of contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, a simultaneous drop in real consumer credit and industrial production accelerates credit crunch dynamics, predicting strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with credit contraction
failure_condition: Forward returns do not significantly differ from baseline when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and RSAFS/INDPRO fall, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no predictive power for 60-day equity returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.0 percent year-over-year growth, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing restrictive policy that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in the majority of high-inflation observations, or the forward return differential between high and low CPI regimes is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of payroll employment and housing construction signals robust labor demand and housing market vitality, which historically precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of PAYEMS and HOUST fails to predict positive SPX returns, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress where industrial production stagnates while the federal funds rate remains elevated above 3.5 percent, stagflationary risks materialize, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not systematically decline when INDPRO stagnates alongside high rates, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.
